title: ModelWisdom: An Integrated Toolkit for TLA+ Model Visualization, Digest and Repair
authors: Zhiyong Chen, Jialun Cao, Chang Xu, Shing-Chi Cheung
abstract: Model checking in TLA+ provides strong correctness guarantees, yet practitioners continue to face significant challenges in interpreting counterexamples, understanding large state-transition graphs, and repairing faulty models. These difficulties stem from the limited explainability of raw model-checker output and the substantial manual effort required to trace violations back to source specifications. Although the TLA+ Toolbox includes a state diagram viewer, it offers only a static, fully expanded graph without folding, color highlighting, or semantic explanations, which limits its scalability and interpretability. We present ModelWisdom, an interactive environment that uses visualization and large language models to make TLA+ model checking more interpretable and actionable. ModelWisdom offers: (i) Model Visualization, with colorized violation highlighting, click-through links from transitions to TLA+ code, and mapping between violating states and broken properties; (ii) Graph Optimization, including tree-based structuring and node/edge folding to manage large models; (iii) Model Digest, which summarizes and explains subgraphs via large language models (LLMs) and performs preprocessing and partial explanations; and (iv) Model Repair, which extracts error information and supports iterative debugging. Together, these capabilities turn raw model-checker output into an interactive, explainable workflow, improving understanding and reducing debugging effort for nontrivial TLA+ specifications. The website to ModelWisdom is available: https://model-wisdom.pages.dev. A demonstrative video can be found at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=plyZo30VShA.
title_zh: ModelWisdom：一种用于TLA+模型可视化、摘要与修复的集成工具包
abstract_zh: TLA+ 中的模型检查能够提供强有力的正确性保证，但实践者在解释反例、理解大规模状态转换图以及修复有缺陷的模型时仍面临显著挑战。这些问题源于模型检查器原始输出的可解释性有限，以及将违规行为追溯到源规格说明所需的巨大人工 effort。尽管 TLA+ Toolbox 包含一个状态图查看器，但它仅提供静态的、完全展开的图，缺乏折叠功能、颜色高亮或语义解释，限制了其可扩展性和可理解性。我们提出了 ModelWisdom，这是一个利用可视化和大语言模型（LLMs）使 TLA+ 模型检查更具可解释性和可操作性的交互式环境。ModelWisdom 提供以下功能：（i）模型可视化，包括违规路径的颜色高亮、从转换到 TLA+ 代码的点击跳转链接，以及违规状态与失效属性之间的映射；（ii）图优化，包括基于树的结构化组织以及节点/边的折叠，以管理大型模型；（iii）模型摘要（Model Digest），利用大语言模型对子图进行总结和解释，并执行预处理和部分解释；（iv）模型修复（Model Repair），提取错误信息并支持迭代式调试。这些功能共同将模型检查器的原始输出转化为一个交互式、可解释的工作流，显著提升了对复杂 TLA+ 规格说明的理解能力，并减少了调试工作量。ModelWisdom 网站地址为：https://model-wisdom.pages.dev。演示视频可访问：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=plyZo30VShA。

title: Automated Test Suite Enhancement Using Large Language Models with Few-shot Prompting
abstract: Unit testing is essential for verifying the functional correctness of code modules (e.g., classes, methods), but manually writing unit tests is often labor-intensive and time-consuming. Unit tests generated by tools that employ traditional approaches, such as search-based software testing (SBST), lack readability, naturalness, and practical usability. LLMs have recently provided promising results and become integral to developers' daily practices. Consequently, software repositories now include a mix of human-written tests, LLM-generated tests, and those from tools employing traditional approaches such as SBST. While LLMs' zero-shot capabilities have been widely studied, their few-shot learning potential for unit test generation remains underexplored. Few-shot prompting enables LLMs to learn from examples in the prompt, and automatically retrieving such examples could enhance test suites. This paper empirically investigates how few-shot prompting with different test artifact sources, comprising human, SBST, or LLM, affects the quality of LLM-generated unit tests as program comprehension artifacts and their contribution to improving existing test suites by evaluating not only correctness and coverage but also readability, cognitive complexity, and maintainability in hybrid human-AI codebases. We conducted experiments on HumanEval and ClassEval datasets using GPT-4o, which is integrated into GitHub Copilot and widely used among developers. We also assessed retrieval-based methods for selecting relevant examples. Our results show that LLMs can generate high-quality tests via few-shot prompting, with human-written examples producing the best coverage and correctness. Additionally, selecting examples based on the combined similarity of problem description and code consistently yields the most effective few-shot prompts.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型与少样本提示的自动化测试套件增强
abstract_zh: 单元测试对于验证代码模块（例如类、方法）的功能正确性至关重要，但手动编写单元测试通常费时费力。采用传统方法（如基于搜索的软件测试，SBST）的工具生成的单元测试往往缺乏可读性、自然性和实际可用性。近年来，大语言模型（LLMs）取得了令人瞩目的成果，并已逐渐融入开发者的日常实践。因此，当前的软件仓库中混合了人工编写的测试、LLM生成的测试，以及采用SBST等传统方法的工具生成的测试。尽管LLM的零样本能力已被广泛研究，但其在单元测试生成中的少样本学习潜力仍缺乏深入探索。少样本提示（few-shot prompting）使LLM能够从提示中的示例中学习，而自动检索合适的示例有望进一步提升测试套件的质量。本文通过实证研究，探讨了使用不同来源的测试构件（包括人工编写、SBST生成或LLM生成）作为少样本提示时，对LLM生成的单元测试质量的影响，以及这些测试作为程序理解构件在混合型人机协同代码库中对现有测试套件的改进作用。评估维度不仅包括正确性和覆盖率，还涵盖可读性、认知复杂度和可维护性。我们在HumanEval和ClassEval数据集上使用GPT-4o（集成于GitHub Copilot且被开发者广泛使用）进行了实验，并评估了基于检索的方法在选择相关示例方面的效果。实验结果表明，通过少样本提示，LLM能够生成高质量的测试，其中以人工编写的示例作为提示时，生成的测试在覆盖率和正确性方面表现最佳。此外，结合问题描述与代码的相似性进行示例选择，能够持续生成最有效的少样本提示。

title: Partial GFlowNet: Accelerating Convergence in Large State Spaces via Strategic Partitioning
authors: Xuan Yu, Xu Wang, Rui Zhu, Yudong Zhang, Yang Wang
abstract: Generative Flow Networks (GFlowNets) have shown promising potential to generate high-scoring candidates with probability proportional to their rewards. As existing GFlowNets freely explore in state space, they encounter significant convergence challenges when scaling to large state spaces. Addressing this issue, this paper proposes to restrict the exploration of actor. A planner is introduced to partition the entire state space into overlapping partial state spaces. Given their limited size, these partial state spaces allow the actor to efficiently identify subregions with higher rewards. A heuristic strategy is introduced to switch partial regions thus preventing the actor from wasting time exploring fully explored or low-reward partial regions. By iteratively exploring these partial state spaces, the actor learns to converge towards the high-reward subregions within the entire state space. Experiments on several widely used datasets demonstrate that \modelname converges faster than existing works on large state spaces. Furthermore, \modelname not only generates candidates with higher rewards but also significantly improves their diversity.
title_zh: 部分GFlowNet：通过策略性划分加速在大状态空间中的收敛
abstract_zh: 生成流网络（Generative Flow Networks, GFlowNets）在以与奖励成比例的概率生成高分候选样本方面展现出良好的潜力。然而，现有的GFlowNets在状态空间中自由探索，当扩展到大规模状态空间时，会面临显著的收敛困难。针对这一问题，本文提出限制智能体（actor）的探索范围。通过引入一个规划器（planner），将整个状态空间划分为若干重叠的子状态空间。由于这些子状态空间规模有限，智能体能够更高效地识别出奖励较高的子区域。同时，本文设计了一种启发式策略，用于在不同子区域之间切换，从而避免智能体在已充分探索或低奖励的区域中浪费时间。通过在这些子状态空间中进行迭代探索，智能体逐步收敛至整个状态空间中的高奖励子区域。在多个常用数据集上的实验表明，\modelname 在大规模状态空间下比现有方法收敛更快。此外，\modelname 不仅能够生成奖励更高的候选样本，还显著提升了生成结果的多样性。

title: Few-Shot Design Optimization by Exploiting Auxiliary Information
authors: Arjun Mani, Carl Vondrick, Richard Zemel
abstract: Many real-world design problems involve optimizing an expensive black-box function $f(x)$, such as hardware design or drug discovery. Bayesian Optimization has emerged as a sample-efficient framework for this problem. However, the basic setting considered by these methods is simplified compared to real-world experimental setups, where experiments often generate a wealth of useful information. We introduce a new setting where an experiment generates high-dimensional auxiliary information $h(x)$ along with the performance measure $f(x)$; moreover, a history of previously solved tasks from the same task family is available for accelerating optimization. A key challenge of our setting is learning how to represent and utilize $h(x)$ for efficiently solving new optimization tasks beyond the task history. We develop a novel approach for this setting based on a neural model which predicts $f(x)$ for unseen designs given a few-shot context containing observations of $h(x)$. We evaluate our method on two challenging domains, robotic hardware design and neural network hyperparameter tuning, and introduce a novel design problem and large-scale benchmark for the former. On both domains, our method utilizes auxiliary feedback effectively to achieve more accurate few-shot prediction and faster optimization of design tasks, significantly outperforming several methods for multi-task optimization.
title_zh: 利用辅助信息进行少样本设计优化
abstract_zh: 许多现实世界的设计问题涉及优化一个昂贵的黑箱函数 $f(x)$，例如硬件设计或药物发现。贝叶斯优化已成为解决此类问题的一种样本高效的框架。然而，这些方法所考虑的基本设定相较于现实世界的实验环境仍较为简化，因为在实际实验中通常会产生大量有用的信息。我们提出了一种新的设定：每次实验不仅产生性能度量 $f(x)$，还会生成高维的辅助信息 $h(x)$；此外，还提供来自同一任务族的先前已解任务的历史记录，以加速新任务的优化过程。我们设定中的一个关键挑战是如何学习表示和利用 $h(x)$，从而在已有任务历史的基础上，高效地求解新的优化任务。为此，我们开发了一种基于神经网络的新方法，该模型能够根据包含少量 $h(x)$ 观测值的上下文（few-shot context），预测未见过的设计方案的 $f(x)$。我们在两个具有挑战性的领域——机器人硬件设计和神经网络超参数调优——上评估了我们的方法，并为前者构建了一个新颖的设计问题和大规模基准测试。在这两个领域中，我们的方法有效利用了辅助反馈，在少样本预测准确性以及设计任务的优化速度方面均显著优于多种多任务优化方法。

title: Benchmarking for Single Feature Attribution with Microarchitecture Cliffs
authors: Hao Zhen, Qingxuan Kang, Yungang Bao, Trevor E. Carlson
abstract: Architectural simulators play a critical role in early microarchitectural exploration due to their flexibility and high productivity. However, their effectiveness is often constrained by fidelity: simulators may deviate from the behavior of the final RTL, leading to unreliable performance estimates. Consequently, model calibration, which aligns simulator behavior with the RTL as the ground-truth microarchitecture, becomes essential for achieving accurate performance modeling. To facilitate model calibration accuracy, we propose Microarchitecture Cliffs, a benchmark generation methodology designed to expose mismatches in microarchitectural behavior between the simulator and RTL. After identifying the key architectural components that require calibration, the Cliff methodology enables precise attribution of microarchitectural differences to a single microarchitectural feature through a set of benchmarks. In addition, we develop a set of automated tools to improve the efficiency of the Cliff workflow. We apply the Cliff methodology to calibrate the XiangShan version of gem5 (XS-GEM5) against the XiangShan open-source CPU (XS-RTL). We reduce the performance error of XS-GEM5 from 59.2% to just 1.4% on the Cliff benchmarks. Meanwhile, the calibration guided by Cliffs effectively reduces the relative error of a representative tightly coupled microarchitectural feature by 48.03%. It also substantially lowers the absolute performance error, with reductions of 15.1% and 21.0% on SPECint2017 and SPECfp2017, respectively.
title_zh: 基于微架构拐点的单特征归因基准测试
abstract_zh: 架构模拟器由于其灵活性和高生产效率，在早期微架构探索中发挥着关键作用。然而，其有效性常常受到保真度的限制：模拟器的行为可能与最终的寄存器传输级（RTL）设计存在偏差，从而导致性能评估结果不可靠。因此，模型校准变得至关重要，即通过将模拟器行为与作为真实微架构基准的RTL对齐，以实现准确的性能建模。为了提升模型校准的准确性，我们提出了“微架构悬崖”（Microarchitecture Cliffs）方法，这是一种用于暴露模拟器与RTL之间微架构行为差异的基准生成方法。在识别出需要校准的关键架构组件后，“悬崖”方法通过一组专门设计的基准程序，能够精确地将微架构差异归因于单一的微架构特性。此外，我们开发了一套自动化工具，以提高“悬崖”工作流程的效率。我们将“悬崖”方法应用于XiangShan版本的gem5模拟器（XS-GEM5）相对于XiangShan开源CPU（XS-RTL）的校准中，成功将XS-GEM5在“悬崖”基准上的性能误差从59.2%降低至仅1.4%。同时，在“悬崖”方法指导下的校准使一个典型紧耦合微架构特性的相对误差降低了48.03%，并在绝对性能误差方面取得了显著改善，在SPECint2017和SPECfp2017上的性能误差分别降低了15.1%和21.0%。

title: Causality-aware Safety Testing for Autonomous Driving Systems
authors: Wenbing Tang, Mingfei Cheng, Renzhi Wang, Yuan Zhou, Chengwei Liu, Yang Liu, Zuohua Ding
abstract: Simulation-based testing is essential for evaluating the safety of Autonomous Driving Systems (ADSs). Comprehensive evaluation requires testing across diverse scenarios that can trigger various types of violations under different conditions. While existing methods typically focus on individual diversity metrics, such as input scenarios, ADS-generated motion commands, and system violations, they often fail to capture the complex interrelationships among these elements. For instance, identical motion commands can produce different collision risks in varying scenes, and the same collision may result from different commands under different scenarios. This oversight leads to gaps in testing coverage, potentially missing critical issues in the ADS under evaluation. In this paper, we propose <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace>, the first causality-aware fuzzing technique that enables efficient and comprehensive testing of ADSs by constructing causal graphs to model the interrelationships among scenarios, actions, and violations. Unlike existing methods that treat diversity metrics independently, we recognize these elements are causally interconnected and use their relationships to identify more diverse violations triggered by fundamentally different causal mechanisms. Specifically, <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> proposes (1) a causality-based feedback mechanism that quantifies the combined diversity of test scenarios by assessing whether they activate new causal relationships, and (2) a causality-driven mutation strategy that prioritizes mutations on input scenario elements with higher causal impact on ego action changes and violation occurrence to enable interpretable and efficient test generation. We evaluated <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> on an industry-grade ADS Apollo, with a high-fidelity simulator LGSVL. Our empirical results demonstrate that <monospace xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">Causal-Fuzzer</monospace> significantly outperforms existing methods in (1) identifying a greater diversity of violations (96.5 violations on average, compared to 66.9 for the best baseline method), (2) providing enhanced testing sufficiency with improved coverage of causal relationships (13.6 unique sceneaction- violation patterns on average, compared to 8.6 for the best baseline method), and (3) achieving greater efficiency in detecting critical scenarios, strong robustness under noise conditions, and good generalizability across varying scenario complexities and violation types. Our source code and experimental results are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://sites.google.com/view/causal-fuzzer</uri>.
title_zh: 面向自动驾驶系统的因果性感知安全测试
abstract_zh:
基于仿真的测试对于评估自动驾驶系统（ADS）的安全性至关重要。全面的评估需要在多种多样的场景下进行测试，以在不同条件下触发各种类型的违规行为。现有的方法通常关注单一的多样性指标，例如输入场景、ADS生成的运动指令以及系统违规等，但往往忽略了这些要素之间复杂的相互关系。例如，相同的运动指令在不同场景中可能带来不同的碰撞风险，而同样的碰撞可能在不同情境下由不同的控制指令导致。这种忽视会导致测试覆盖上的盲区，可能遗漏被测ADS中的关键问题。

本文提出了Causal-Fuzzer，这是首个具备因果感知能力的模糊测试技术，通过构建因果图来建模场景、行为与违规之间的相互关系，从而实现对ADS高效且全面的测试。与现有方法将多样性指标独立看待不同，我们认识到这些要素在因果关系上是相互关联的，并利用这些关系识别出由根本不同因果机制所触发的更多样化的违规行为。具体而言，Causal-Fuzzer提出了：（1）一种基于因果关系的反馈机制，通过评估测试场景是否激活了新的因果关系，来量化测试场景的整体多样性；（2）一种因果驱动的变异策略，优先对在因果上对自车行为变化和违规发生具有更高影响的输入场景元素进行变异，从而实现可解释且高效的测试用例生成。

我们在工业级自动驾驶系统Apollo上，结合高保真仿真器LGSVL对Causal-Fuzzer进行了评估。实验结果表明，Causal-Fuzzer在以下方面显著优于现有方法：（1）能够发现更多样化的违规行为（平均发现96.5个违规，而最优基线方法为66.9个）；（2）提供更强的测试充分性，显著提升了对因果关系的覆盖（平均发现13.6种独特的“场景-行为-违规”模式，优于基线方法的8.6种）；（3）在检测关键场景方面效率更高，具备更强的抗噪声鲁棒性，并在不同复杂度的场景和多种违规类型下表现出良好的泛化能力。我们的源代码和实验结果已公开，详见：https://sites.google.com/view/causal-fuzzer。

title: Efficient VLSI Implementation of 256 bit AES-Advanced Encryption Standard
authors: Tharun Tejas G, B N Shobha, Naveen K B
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Emerging Research in Electronics, Computer Science and Technology (ICERECT)
abstract: This paper presents the design, simulation, and FPGA synthesis of a 256-bit Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) encryption core, implemented in Verilog HDL. The design was verified using Intel’s ModelSim simulator and synthesized using Intel Quartus Prime targeting the Cyclone V FPGA. AES-256, known for its high security, is realized in a hardware-efficient, resource-conscious manner for embedded cryptographic applications.The proposed architecture integrates all standard AES transformations—SubBytes, ShiftRows, MixColumns, and AddRound-Key—along with a dynamic 256-bit key expansion mechanism. The design employs a Finite State Machine (FSM)-controlled, single-round iterative approach, reusing one AES round module across all 14 encryption rounds. This reduces hardware overhead, routing complexity, and power consumption, while retaining functional correctness and scalability.Although inherently iterative, the architecture adopts pipeline-aware modularization principles through structured FSM timing and round-stage encapsulation. This hybrid strategy enables efficient resource usage and maintains design clarity, drawing from pipelining methodology without implementing full parallel stages.To ensure flexibility and deployment suitability, the AES core is mapped onto Cyclone V FPGA, demonstrating favorable resource utilization, timing closure, and operational latency, validating its use in real-time, low-power cryptographic systems.
title_zh: 256位AES高级加密标准的高效VLSI实现
abstract_zh:
本文提出了一种256位高级加密标准（AES）加密核心的设计、仿真与FPGA综合方案，采用Verilog HDL实现。该设计使用Intel的ModelSim仿真器进行功能验证，并利用Intel Quartus Prime工具针对Cyclone V FPGA进行综合。AES-256以其高安全性著称，本设计以硬件高效、资源节约的方式实现，适用于嵌入式密码学应用。

所提出的架构集成了所有标准AES变换——字节替换（SubBytes）、行移位（ShiftRows）、列混淆（MixColumns）和轮密钥加（AddRoundKey），并包含一个动态的256位密钥扩展机制。设计采用由有限状态机（FSM）控制的单轮迭代结构，通过复用同一个AES轮运算模块完成全部14轮加密过程。这种方法显著降低了硬件开销、布线复杂度和功耗，同时保持了功能正确性和可扩展性。

尽管本质上为迭代结构，本架构通过结构化的FSM时序控制和轮次阶段封装，引入了面向流水线的模块化设计原则。这种混合策略在不实现完全并行流水级的前提下，实现了高效的资源利用并保持了设计的清晰性。

为确保灵活性和实际部署适用性，该AES核心被映射到Cyclone V FPGA上，实验结果表明其在资源利用率、时序收敛性和操作延迟方面表现良好，验证了其在实时、低功耗密码系统中的适用性。

title: Combinatorial Security Testing—10 Years Later
authors: Dimitris E. Simos, Manuel Leithner, Rick Kuhn, Bernhard Garn, Raghu Kacker, Jeff Lei
abstract: Ten years ago, we introduced a research program applying high strength combinatorial test methods to vulnerability and fault detection for cybersecurity. This article discusses advances in the field and where these methods may be effective for today’s complex security problems.
title_zh: 组合安全测试——十年之后
abstract_zh: 十年前，我们启动了一项研究计划，将高强度组合测试方法应用于网络安全领域的漏洞和故障检测。本文讨论了该领域的发展进展，以及这些方法在应对当今复杂安全问题时可能发挥有效作用的方向。

title: Co-EDR: Cooperative Event-Driven Fuzzing for Efficient Vulnerability Detection in Firmware
authors: Seung-Ha Jee, So-Eun Jeon, Il-Gu Lee
abstract: With the Internet of Things (IoT) becoming increasingly integrated into everyday life and industry, ensuring efficient and secure data processing in large-scale IoT networks is critical. Typically, vulnerabilities in wireless firmware are patched via over-the-air updates. However, timely patching remains a challenge. Efficient fuzzing techniques have been proposed to address this problem, but conventional fuzzing techniques often rely on random input generation, resulting in poor code coverage, high overhead, and limited detection performance because of the exhaustive injection of all possible input combinations. To address these limitations, this study proposes a cooperative event data recording (Co-EDR) technique to efficiently verify firmware stability in wireless communication systems. Co-EDR improves fuzzing efficiency by recording event data that previously triggered crashes and reusing these data as inputs. In addition, it uses cooperative fuzzing across multiple devices to improve code coverage and vulnerability detection. Experimental results indicate that Co-EDR results in an 8.52-fold reduction in the time required to achieve the same vulnerability detection rate as conventional random fuzzing, using approximately 2.47 times less memory and enhances the fuzzing efficiency by up to 32.5 times. These improvements enable expedited vulnerability detection and timely firmware patching with reduced resource expenditure compared with conventional techniques, rendering the proposed method a practical solution for improving the security of IoT systems.
title_zh: Co-EDR：面向固件高效漏洞检测的协作式事件驱动模糊测试
abstract_zh: 随着物联网（IoT）在日常生活和工业中的应用日益广泛，确保大规模物联网网络中数据处理的高效性和安全性变得至关重要。通常，无线固件中的漏洞通过空中下载（OTA）更新进行修补。然而，及时修补仍然是一个挑战。虽然已有研究提出高效的模糊测试技术来应对这一问题，但传统的模糊测试方法通常依赖随机输入生成，由于穷举所有可能的输入组合，导致代码覆盖率低、开销高、检测性能有限。为克服这些局限性，本研究提出了一种协作式事件数据记录（Co-EDR）技术，以高效验证无线通信系统中固件的稳定性。Co-EDR通过记录曾引发崩溃的事件数据，并将其复用为输入，从而提升模糊测试效率。此外，该技术利用多设备间的协同模糊测试，进一步提高代码覆盖率和漏洞检测能力。实验结果表明，与传统的随机模糊测试相比，Co-EDR在实现相同漏洞检测率的情况下，所需时间减少了8.52倍，内存消耗约降低至原来的2.47倍，模糊测试效率最高可提升32.5倍。这些改进使得漏洞检测更加快速，固件修补更加及时，同时显著降低了资源消耗，相较于传统方法更具实用性，为提升物联网系统的安全性提供了一种有效的解决方案。
